items.title: UK Defence spending under scrutiny as former minister warns "Putin will be watching this carefully"
items.description: When he came into office, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer declared that the first duty of a leader is to keep the country safe. It was a stinging letter, claiming Sir Keir was unable to make the financial commitments needed to defend the country against the spectre of a Russian attack. A second defence minister, Al Cairns, also resigned for the same reasons.They had both seen a long-delayed British Defence Plan, which has still not been made public. items.title: Iran: Will a peace deal with the US be reached soon?
items.description: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said a deal aimed at ending the war was closer than "ever before" and expected to be finalized within 24 hours. Pakistan was preparing for the electronic signing of the agreement, to be followed immediately by technical-level talks next week.

items.description: From bubbling sorghum porridge to village-reared chicken and ox-head stew, traditional food restaurants are booming in Zimbabwe’s second city, Bulawayo. Driven by health-conscious consumers and nostalgia for homegrown flavours, indigenous cuisine is making a strong comeback.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Saibari and Vinicius spark draw in Group C drama
items.description: Brazil and Morocco drew 1-1 in their Group C opener at MetLife stadium. Saibari's early lob gave Morocco the lead, but Vinicius Junior's sublime finish levelled the match before half-time. Both sides traded chances in a tense second half, neither able to break the deadlock. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.

items.title: New York Knicks beat Spurs to claim first NBA title in 53 years
items.description: Jalen Brunson scored 45 points as the New York Knicks beat the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 on Saturday to clinch their first NBA championship in 53 years. The Knicks sealed the best-of-seven NBA Finals 4-1, denying Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs on their home court to capture their first title since 1973.

items.title: News24 | Last-minute US Supreme Court order halts Alabama nitrogen gas execution
items.description: The US Supreme Court blocked Alabama from executing a death row inmate by using nitrogen gas, in a rare last-minute intervention after a lower court ruled the method was likely unconstitutional.

items.title: Nigeria: Abducted former general dies in captivity
items.description: Rabe Abubakar was abducted along with his wife by so-called bandits in the northwest. A government statement said he died from complications related to diabetes and hypertension.

items.title: Russian drone barrage hits Kyiv and Mykolaiv as Ukraine reports major fires
items.description: Russian drone strikes hit multiple locations across Ukraine overnight into 12 June, damaging sites near Kyiv and in the Mykolaiv region, injuring one person and triggering a major fire, while Ukrainian air defences said they intercepted most of the 117 drones launched.

items.title: Lebanon reports Israeli strikes in south and east amid broad evacuation warnings
items.description: The Israeli army on Saturday issued evacuation warnings for the southern city of Nabatieh and more than 20 other locations across Lebanon as strikes were reported in the country's south and east. The latest strikes came as the US and Iran indicated they were close to reaching a deal on ending the Middle East war that could also include Lebanon.

items.title: North Korea condemns U.S. missile sale approval to South Korea, KCNA says
